## [00:00 - 01:32]

This is the Pubcast Shot. I'm John Loomer. And today we're going to talk about why you should be ethical to a fault. Let's go. Be ethical to a fault. Don't give anyone a reason to believe you might be doing something unethical. This means doing the right thing as a habit. It also means knowing the rules inside and out. Some of the rules within the Meta-Ed guidelines might not be obvious. Read and know them. Ignorance is not an excuse. It doesn't mean you should venture into these gray areas that can lead to great results. Briefly, at least. Being ethical is a commitment with long term benefits. Bending the rules can have short term benefit but with huge long term risks. You can't afford to have your ad account, Facebook page, or business manager shut down. And I know there are often very innocent people who get caught up in that as well. Increase your odds by simply being ethical to a fault. Know that line that you will not cross, whether it's written or not. Except that some people will cross that line and benefit, at least for now.
